title: Jurisdictional Boundaries: OSHA, FDA, and ANSI
content:
OSHA Jurisdiction:

Federal OSHA has no specific requirement for Laser 'Interlocks', but requires companies under its jurisdiction to follow industry standard 'best practices' for laser safety. Enforcement is through the 'General Duty Clause' of 29 CFR 1910.

Reference Document for Enforcement:
- ANSI Z136.1-2014 and related ANSI Z136 sub-chapters
- If problems are found, OSHA refers to FDA enforcement codes under 21 CFR 1040

FDA Jurisdiction:

The U.S. Food & Drug Administration's Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) mandates are released as federal code in Title 21 of the Code of Federal Regulations (21 CFR).

Key 21 CFR Chapters:
- 21 CFR 1000-1005: Broad scope devices, records/reports, import/export requirements
- 21 CFR 1010: General performance standards, certifications and variances
- 21 CFR 1040.10: Performance standards for manufacture of laser products (interlocks, labeling, power classification)
- 21 CFR 1040.11: Performance standards for specific use laser products (medical, survey, leveling, alignment, demonstration)

ANSI Role:

ANSI Z136.1 provides the 'best practices' that OSHA references for enforcement. State OSHA may or may not specifically address Laser Interlocks depending on the state.
plain_language_summary: Think of FDA as the 'car manufacturer regulator' (makes sure cars are built safely) and OSHA as the 'traffic cop' (makes sure cars are driven safely). ANSI provides the 'rules of the road' that both follow.

FDA_CDRH.enforcement: Product cannot be sold without compliance

Federal_OSHA.enforcement: Citations and fines for unsafe conditions

State_OSHA.note: Some states have stricter requirements than federal

ANSI.enforcement: Referenced by OSHA - becomes effectively mandatory
ANSI.role: Provides the 'best practices' that OSHA enforces

laser_manufacturer.fda_role: Ensures product is designed safely before sale
laser_manufacturer.osha_role: Ensures factory workers are safe while building lasers
laser_manufacturer.result: Must comply with BOTH sets of regulations

nightclub.fda_role: Minimal - just checks product was properly manufactured
nightclub.osha_role: Ensures employees and patrons are safe
nightclub.result: Follows OSHA requirements, references ANSI standards
